hook: You already know how to find missing sides of right triangles using sine, cosine, and tangent — but what happens when angles grow beyond 90°, or when you need to model a wave, a heartbeat, or the swing of a pendulum? Trigonometry has a much bigger story to tell than right triangles alone. In this unit, you'll discover how a simple circle of radius 1 unlocks patterns that repeat forever.
concept: Every topic in this unit connects back to one powerful idea: angles and ratios create predictable, repeating relationships that can describe everything from sound waves to the orbits of planets — formally known as periodic functions defined by trigonometric ratios.

topic_roadmap.description: You will sharpen your ability to find missing sides and angles in right triangles using $\sin$, $\cos$, and $\tan$, building the foundation for everything ahead.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Angles in Standard Position and Radian Measure
topic_roadmap.description: Master how to place any angle on the coordinate plane and convert between degrees and radians so you can work fluently with angles of any size.
Learn to use the unit circle to find exact values of trigonometric functions for key angles, connecting geometry and algebra in one elegant diagram.
Apply transformations — amplitude, period, phase shift, and vertical shift — to graph $y = a\sin(bx + c) + d$ and $y = a\cos(bx + c) + d$ with precision.
Extend your graphing skills to tangent, cotangent, secant, and cosecant, recognizing their unique shapes, asymptotes, and periodic behavior.
You will learn to use inverse trig functions ($\sin^{-1}$, $\cos^{-1}$, $\tan^{-1}$) to recover angle measures from ratio values, understanding their restricted domains and ranges.
Apply the Law of Sines ($\frac{a}{\sin A} = \frac{b}{\sin B}$) and the Law of Cosines ($c^2 = a^2 + b^2 - 2ab\cos C$) to solve any triangle, not just right triangles.
